threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the Overground is a novelty. Locals know it's just another option for the delays, not a sightseeing tour.
Heard a guy on the 47 last night passionately arguing with a pigeon about which way to go. The pigeon just blinked, unfazed. Classic Bermondsey.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a grandma at the Kottbusser Tor U-Bahn platform handing out homemade muffins. That’s when I felt the real spirit of this place.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice how the BVG drivers greet you like old friends? It's a tiny touch, but in Berlin's bustle, it feels oddly personal.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a true local by their reaction when a bus driver stops for a dog on the tracks—eye rolls and a resigned sigh instead of outrage.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 31 with a mime performing his act while we waited at the stop. Only in SF do you get free entertainment while stuck in transit.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the plazas. They miss the tiny bakeries tucked away on side streets, where locals grab their morning calentitos.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by how they stand on the wrong side of the escalator—always blocking traffic like it’s their first day navigating the metro.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You call it a 'bagel shop' instead of a 'beigelry' and order coffee without mentioning oat milk. That's an instant giveaway.
Caught a bloke singing opera on the N137 last night, no one batted an eyelid. Brixton nights can be surreal, even sober.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a toddler painting a mural on the sidewalk while their dad brewed coffee on a camping stove. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Friedrichshain, it's normal to find a spontaneous rave in an abandoned building at 3 AM. The city's unfinished spaces are its life. Totally unique vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a true local would know not to bother with BART during rush hour. You can hear the brakes overheating from two blocks away.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught Muni at Civic Center yesterday; a dog on the tracks had commuters more riled up than the usual delays. Only in SF do furry passengers steal the show.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the parks and plazas, but it's the little corner bakeries at dawn that breathe life into our mornings.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still fumbling with the metro map at Diagonal, clearly convinced they won’t get lost. The locals don’t even glance.
